mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales of autos and light trucks combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary erosion, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and flat CPIAUCSL fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization improvements and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week specifically during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during early-cycle recovery periods identified by NBER dating.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all market regimes over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 230.0, inflation expectations force monetary tightening and compress real corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns exceed zero in more than 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL crosses above 230.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and real GDP growth confirm broad-based economic expansion and credit availability, driving positive equity market performance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and GDPC1 uptrend fails to generate positive 60-day returns with statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside persistently low federal funds rates indicates monetary policy failure to stimulate growth, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5
failure_condition: Markets price in policy accommodation, yielding positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases when unemployment rises with rates below 0.5 percent.
